summary: Enterprises are already deploying hybrid quantum-classical workflows, notably using algorithms like Variational Quantum Eigensolver and Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm, which allow quantum and classical processors to collaborate on complex tasks. IBM’s Load Sharing Facility coordinates workflows across quantum processors and traditional systems, while Quantum Machines’ OPX1000 enables real-time orchestration and quantum feedback within hundreds of nanoseconds, supported by additional acceleration layers. However, operators face challenges from fragmented dashboards, making system health monitoring and troubleshooting difficult. User-centric dashboard designs are proposed to unify job statistics, resource health, and telemetry into streamlined visual timelines and interactive components. Such dashboards enhance transparency, reduce diagnostic time, and expand accessibility of hybrid quantum-classical operations, moving beyond the expertise of physicists.
